ui.articleHintHtml:
Read the article below to explore the science of zoo enrichment.
Interaction: Tap the highlighted words to view definitions and listen to pronunciation.
ui.vocabPlaceholderText: Select a highlighted word to view its analysis.

speech.unsupportedMessage: Sorry, your browser doesn't support text to speech.

article.title: Rethinking Animal Play: The Science of Zoo Enrichment

paragraphs.en: Keeping wild animals in captivity has always been a complex scientific issue. While modern zoos aim to protect species that human activities endanger in the wild, providing a healthy mental state for these animals is highly challenging. In the past, people naturally believed that putting animals in lavish enclosures with plenty of free food would ease their lives and make them happy. However, animal psychologists now confirm that this idea can easily misguide the public. A completely effortless life actually harms an animal’s mental well-being, leading to severe boredom and stress.
paragraphs.zh: 将野生动物圈养(captivity)一直是一个复杂的科学问题。虽然现代动物园旨在保护在野外受到人类活动濒危(endanger)的物种，但为这些动物提供健康的心理状态却极具挑战性。过去，人们自然而然地认为，把动物放在拥有充足免费食物的奢华(lavish)围栏里，会减轻(ease)它们的生活压力并让它们快乐。然而，动物心理学家现在证实(confirm)，这种想法很容易误导(misguide)公众。完全不费吹灰之力的生活实际上会损害动物的心理健康，导致严重的无聊和压力。

paragraphs.en: To alleviate this stress, scientists introduced a concept called "environmental enrichment." The primary goal is to motivate animals to use their natural survival instincts, just as they would in the forest. Surprisingly, the most effective enrichment tools are not expensive technological devices. Zoo keepers often use clean, safe materials that might otherwise go to a local landfill, such as cardboard boxes, old clothes, or plastic pipes. Instead of treating these items as useless trash, caretakers creatively craft them into complex "puzzle feeders".
paragraphs.zh: 为了缓解(alleviate)这种压力，科学家引入了一个叫做“环境丰富化”的概念。主要目标是激发(motivate)动物使用它们自然的生存本能，就像它们在森林里一样。令人惊讶的是，最有效(effective)的丰富化工具并不是昂贵的技术设备。动物园饲养员经常使用可能原本会被送往当地垃圾填埋场(landfill)的干净、安全的材料，比如纸箱、旧衣服或塑料管。看护者并没有将这些物品视为无用的垃圾(trash)，而是创造性地将它们制作(craft)成复杂的“玩具喂食器”。

paragraphs.en: These puzzles require animals to work for their meals. For example, monkeys might be given a wooden box where they must use sticks to probe hidden holes to find sweet treats. This mental workout keeps their brains active. Furthermore, social learning plays a huge role. When one clever individual figures out how to open a puzzle, its peers will watch closely and copy the behavior. This social interaction not only passes on knowledge but also strengthens group loyalty and emotional bonds, perfectly mirroring how wild groups operate.
paragraphs.zh: 这些谜题要求动物为它们的食物付出努力。例如，猴子可能会得到一个木箱，它们必须用棍子去探测(probe)隐藏的洞才能找到甜食。这种脑力锻炼使它们的大脑保持活跃。此外，社会学习也发挥着巨大作用。当一个聪明的个体弄清楚如何打开谜题时，它的同伴(peers)会密切注视并模仿这种行为。这种社会互动不仅传递了知识，而且加强了群体忠诚度(loyalty)和情感纽带，完美地反映了野生群体的运作方式。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately, allowing animals to take charge of their own daily activities makes them significantly healthier. It proves a counter-intuitive philosophical point: a perfect, stress-free life without challenges is not always a good life. By introducing small, manageable difficulties, scientists help captive animals maintain their wild and brilliant minds.
paragraphs.zh: 最终，允许动物掌控(charge)自己的日常活动使它们变得更加健康。它证明了一个反直觉的哲学观点：没有挑战的完美、无压力的生活并不总是好生活。通过引入小的、可控的困难，科学家帮助被圈养的动物保持它们狂野而聪明的大脑。

captivity.def: The condition of being imprisoned or confined.

endanger.def: Put (someone or something) at risk of danger.

ease.def: Make (something unpleasant or intense) less serious or severe.

confirm.def: Establish the truth or correctness of.

alleviate.def: Make (suffering, deficiency, or a problem) less severe.

motivate.def: Provide (someone) with a motive for doing something.

effective.def: Successful in producing a desired or intended result.

landfill.def: A place to dispose of refuse and other waste material.

probe.def: Physically explore or examine (something) with the hands or an instrument.

peers.def: A person of the same age, status, or ability as another specified person.

loyalty.def: A strong feeling of support or allegiance.

charge.def: Responsibility for the care or control of someone or something.
charge.trans: 负责，掌控 (如: take charge of)

puzzle feeders.def: Devices that require animals to solve a problem or perform a task to obtain food, used for environmental enrichment.
puzzle feeders.trans: 玩具喂食器，益智喂食器

chart.title: Impact of Enrichment on Captive Animals
chart.description: Comparison of animal well-being indicators between standard lavish enclosures (free food) and enriched environments (puzzle feeders).

insights.title: Mental Stimulation
insights.text: Puzzle feeders significantly boost brain activity by forcing animals to use their natural instincts.

insights.title: Stress Reduction
insights.text: An effortless life leads to high stress, whereas manageable challenges dramatically reduce boredom.

quiz.title: Knowledge Check
quiz.description: Test your comprehension of the reading passage.

questions.statement: Providing captive animals with an effortless life in lavish enclosures is a scientifically proven way to improve their mental health.

rationale.en: Paragraph A states: 'A completely effortless life actually harms an animal’s mental well-being...' The statement contradicts the text.
rationale.zh: 【反义词陷阱】原文 Paragraph A 明确指出“完全不费吹灰之力的生活实际上会损害 (harms) 动物的心理健康”，而题目中使用了 improve (改善)，意思完全相反。

questions.statement: Monkeys are generally faster at solving puzzle feeders than other social animals in zoos.

rationale.en: Paragraph C mentions monkeys solving puzzle feeders and social animals copying behavior, but it never compares the speed between monkeys and other animals.
rationale.zh: 【无中生有】原文 Paragraph C 提到了猴子 (monkeys) 和解谜 (solving puzzle feeders)，但绝对没有比较“猴子比其他动物更快 (faster)”。

questions.prompt: According to Paragraph A, what is the modern scientific view on giving animals 'free food'?

options.text: It effectively eases the daily lives of animals, making them behave naturally.
It misguides animals into forgetting how to interact with their peers.
It is less beneficial for an animal's psychological health than previously thought.
It is the primary reason why certain wild species become endangered.

rationale.en: Paragraph A explains that scientists confirm the idea of free food easing lives misguides the public, and it actually harms mental well-being. 'Less beneficial' matches 'harms'.
rationale.zh: 【同义替换】正确项C 'less beneficial' 完美对应了原文的 'harms' 和打破过去的错误认知。干扰项A包含原词 'ease'，干扰项B包含原词 'misguide'，但均属于偷换概念。

questions.instruction: Complete the summary below. Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questions.contentTemplate:
Social Learning in Zoos

Puzzle feeders do more than just feed animals; they encourage social behavior. When an animal successfully solves a puzzle, other individuals, known as their {{blank1}}, observe and imitate this success. Researchers note that this shared activity is highly beneficial, as it builds deeper emotional connections and increases group {{blank2}} within the animal community.

rationale.en: For blank 1, 'other individuals' matches 'its peers' in Paragraph C. For blank 2, 'increases group' matches 'strengthens group loyalty' in Paragraph C.
rationale.zh: 【语法与替换解析】第一空：题目 'other individuals, known as their...' 对应原文 'its peers'（它的同伴）。第二空：题目 'increases group ______' 对应原文 'strengthens group loyalty'，strengthen 等于 increase，所以填 loyalty。
